---
title: "scRNA-seq Bao's blog"
subtitle: "From single-cell concepts to practical R analysis"
---

## Welcome

This blog is designed for beginners who want to understand single-cell RNA-seq from both the biological and computational sides.

The blog starts with a non-coding orientation day, then moves into R foundations and practical single-cell analysis.

## Blog structure

| Phase | Focus | Output |
|---|---|---|
| Orientation | Big picture of scRNA-seq | People can draw the full pipeline |
| Day 0 | R setup and basic R objects | People can run R scripts |
| Weeks 1-4 | Data structure, QC, normalization, PCA/UMAP | First complete Seurat workflow |
| Weeks 5-6 | Clustering, markers, annotation, integration | Biological interpretation |
| Weeks 7-8 | DE, pseudotime preview, mini-project | Final report |

## Start here

1. Read the [Orientation lesson](orientation/index.qmd).
2. Follow the [Setup page](setup.qmd).
3. Prepare for [Day 0: R foundation](day0/index.qmd).
